headlines.title: Watch: Met Police body-worn footage of Golders Green arrest
headlines.description: Footage shows the moment a man was arrested after two Jewish men were stabbed in north London.

headlines.title: Oil price jumps after report Trump to be given new Iran options
headlines.description: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

headlines.title: 'We will kill you and burn your house': Council staff under attack from High Street gangs
headlines.description: Dozens of Trading Standards officers describe intimidation from criminals running mini-marts and vape shops.

headlines.title: Have the royals got their mojo back from US visit?
headlines.description: Many commentators, even critics of the monarchy, seem delighted at the spectacle.

headlines.title: Statue with Banksy's signature appears in London
headlines.description: A sculpture of a man marching off a plinth in St James's appears to bear the artist's name.

headlines.title: Endometriosis could be diagnosed earlier with new scan technique
headlines.description: A new scan technique could spot areas of endometriosis missed by conventional scans, scientists say.

headlines.title: The city caught in the middle of the big energy shift debate
headlines.description: Is the UK fumbling the shift away from oil and gas jobs?

headlines.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
headlines.description: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

headlines.title: Interest rates expected to be held as uncertainty over Iran war continues
headlines.description: Future base rate changes are hard to predict as analysts judge the economic impact of the Iran war.

headlines.title: Video that police denied existed shows woman pinned down
headlines.description: Nadine Buzzard-Quashie speaks for first time about her "David and Goliath battle" against two police forces.

headlines.title: Antisemitism 'a national security emergency', government terror adviser says
headlines.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood says the issue is being treated as an "absolute priority" but does not agree it constitutes a national emergency.

headlines.title: 'We were first on scene' The Jewish security volunteers who rushed to help
headlines.description: "We got a phone call to the hotline saying that there's a man stabbing people," a Shomrim member says.

headlines.title: What we know about the attack
headlines.description: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, an attack police have declared a terrorist incident.

headlines.title: 'I did not expect it': Kenya's Sabastian Sawe welcomed home with jubilant celebrations
headlines.description: The first man to run a marathon in under two hours in a competitive race received a hero's welcome from supporters and family when he arrived home.

headlines.title: Legal experts sceptical of DoJ case against Comey over seashell post
headlines.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche said the case was investigated for months, adding "it's serious when you threaten the president."

headlines.title: 'Watershed' moment as communities gain new rights to buy valuable local buildings
headlines.description: Ministers say the new law in England gives power to local people who want to help others.

headlines.title: A son overlooked and a jailed tycoon: Inside Samsung's succession drama
headlines.description: The family dynasty behind Samsung is so complicated it regularly makes headline news in South Korea.

headlines.title: The 'Polar Bear Capital' with Arctic gateway ambitions
headlines.description: Canada is weighing whether a year-round northern trade route to Europe from Churchill, Manitoba, is viable, as climate change speeds Arctic warming.

headlines.title: Smiles and wonder: How the US reacted to King Charles
headlines.description: A divided US has come together to watch King Charles' visit with a mix of awe, delight and hope.

headlines.title: The subtle messages hidden in the King's speeches
headlines.description: How King Charles III navigated a diplomatic tightrope in his speech to the US Congress.

headlines.title: In pictures: Fashion royalty meets British royalty
headlines.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla continued their state visit to the US by attending a ceremony at the National September 11 Memorial in New York City.

headlines.title: What the royals did on their third day in the US
headlines.description: After visiting the 9/11 Memorial, Queen Camilla read to children at the New York Public Library and King Charles visited a community organisation in Harlem.

headlines.title: King and Queen lay flowers at 9/11 Memorial in New York
headlines.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla on their state visit to the US visit the memorial in New York.

headlines.title: 'Attacked 28 times in a day' - BBC visits heavily targeted US-UK base in Iraq
headlines.description: Before the fragile ceasefire in the region, this was one of the most heavily targeted US and UK military bases in the Middle East.

headlines.title: Hereditary peers' last hurrah as 700-year-old system abolished
headlines.description: It comes after legislation to remove their right to sit in the upper chamber passed last month.

headlines.title: Premier Inn owner to cut 3,800 jobs in savings plan
headlines.description: Whitbread says it will also remodel its 197 hotel restaurants as part of a five-year savings plan.

headlines.title: Saudi Arabia to stop funding LIV Golf next season
headlines.description: Saudi Arabia will withdraw its multi-billion dollar backing of LIV Golf at the end of the season, plunging the future of the series into doubt.

headlines.title: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, after Merz criticises war
headlines.description: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

headlines.title: Ukraine volunteer claims MP stole idea to donate ventilators - to divert them to Cuba
headlines.description: Steve Witherden MP has been accused of plagiarism over a letter written to him about the ventilators.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: What The King Did (And Didn’t) Say To Trump
headlines.description: Decoding what the King said to President Trump.

headlines.title: Who is Trump’s 'would-be assassin' and were there security failings at DC shooting?
headlines.description: The US president was evacuated from the White House Correspondents Dinner

headlines.title: Parker leaves Burnley after Premier League relegation
headlines.description: Burnley head coach Scott Parker leaves the club following talks with the club's hierarchy.

headlines.title: Saudi Arabia to stop funding LIV Golf at end of season
headlines.description: Saudi Arabia will withdraw its multi-billion dollar backing of LIV Golf at the end of the season, plunging the future of the series into doubt.

headlines.title: New Arsenal deal for England captain Williamson
headlines.description: Leah Williamson is the fourth player to commit her future to the club in recent weeks, with Kim Little, Stina Blackstenius, and Steph Catley also signing new deals.

headlines.title: Lomu, Wilkinson, Pollock? Rugby's search for a crossover star
headlines.description: Henry Pollock has signed with Eddie Hearn's talent agency in the hope of boosting his profile, but why has rugby found it so hard to forge mainstream figureheads?

headlines.title: Pochettino sad to see Spurs suffering
headlines.description: Tottenham's Premier League relegation struggles are "difficult to accept", says former manager Mauricio Pochettino.

headlines.title: Were Arsenal right to be 'fuming' with refereeing after Atletico draw?
headlines.description: Mikel Arteta was left "fuming" with the officials after Arsenal's draw at Atletico Madrid - what did they get right and wrong?

headlines.title: Murphy to face Higgins after dashing Zhao's Crucible hopes
headlines.description: Shaun Murphy dashes Zhao Xintong's hopes of lifting the Crucible curse with a superb 13-10 win as he moves into the semi-finals of the World Championship.
